label: Set Theory
shortDescription: The foundation of modern mathematics: sets, ordinals, cardinals, and independence.
modulesDescription: Read the set theory chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogTitle: Learn Set Theory for Free | CalcPath
ogDescription: Master naive and axiomatic set theory, Zermelo-Fraenkel axioms, ordinals, cardinals, the axiom of choice and its equivalents, forcing, independence results, and advanced topics like large cardinals and Martin's axiom — with ELI5 explanations, worked examples, and practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete set theory course covering foundations (sets, elements, subsets, unions), the ZFC axioms, ordinals and cardinals, transfinite methods, the axiom of choice and equivalents (Zorn, well-ordering, Tychonoff), independence and forcing, and advanced topics in descriptive set theory and forcing axioms. Features ELI5 intuition, step-by-step worked examples, common mistakes, and practice problems for each lesson.

topics.title: Set (mathematics)
topics.description: A set is a well-defined collection of distinct objects, the fundamental building block of mathematics.

topics.title: Element (mathematics)
topics.description: An element is an object that belongs to a set, denoted by the membership relation ∈.

topics.title: Subset
topics.description: A subset is a set whose elements are all contained in another set, written A ⊆ B.

topics.title: Power set
topics.description: The power set of A is the set of all subsets of A, with cardinality 2^|A|.

topics.title: Union (set theory)
topics.description: The union of sets combines all elements from the given sets into one, denoted A ∪ B.

topics.title: Intersection (set theory)
topics.description: The intersection of sets contains only the elements common to all of them, denoted A ∩ B.

topics.title: Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory
topics.description: Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory (ZF) is the standard axiomatic foundation for set theory and most of mathematics.

topics.title: Axiom of extensionality
topics.description: The axiom of extensionality states that two sets are equal if and only if they have exactly the same elements.

topics.title: Axiom of regularity
topics.description: The axiom of regularity (foundation) prevents infinite descending membership chains, ensuring well-founded sets.

topics.title: Axiom of infinity
topics.description: The axiom of infinity asserts the existence of an infinite set, allowing construction of the natural numbers.

topics.title: Axiom of power set
topics.description: The axiom of power set guarantees that for any set A, its power set P(A) exists as a set.

topics.title: Axiom schema of replacement
topics.description: The axiom schema of replacement allows substituting elements via definable functions while preserving set-hood.

topics.title: Ordinal number
topics.description: Ordinal numbers extend natural numbers to describe well-ordered sets and their order types.

topics.title: Cardinal number
topics.description: Cardinal numbers measure the size of sets, with infinite cardinals like ℵ₀ and 𝔠.

topics.title: Transfinite induction
topics.description: Transfinite induction generalizes mathematical induction to well-ordered sets beyond the naturals.

topics.title: Well-ordering theorem
topics.description: The well-ordering theorem states that every set can be well-ordered, equivalent to the axiom of choice.

topics.title: Aleph number
topics.description: Aleph numbers ℵ_α are the infinite cardinal numbers in increasing order of size.

topics.title: Continuum hypothesis
topics.description: The continuum hypothesis asserts that there is no set whose cardinality is strictly between that of the naturals and the reals.

topics.title: Axiom of choice
topics.description: The axiom of choice states that for any collection of nonempty sets, there exists a choice function selecting one element from each.

topics.title: Zorn's lemma
topics.description: Zorn's lemma says that a partially ordered set with upper bounds for every chain has a maximal element; equivalent to AC.

topics.title: Well-ordering theorem
topics.description: In the context of the axiom of choice, the well-ordering theorem asserts every set admits a well-ordering.

topics.title: Tychonoff's theorem
topics.description: Tychonoff's theorem states that the product of any collection of compact spaces is compact; equivalent to the axiom of choice.

topics.title: Forcing (mathematics)
topics.description: Forcing is a technique invented by Cohen to prove independence results by adding generic sets to models of set theory.

topics.title: Independence (mathematical logic)
topics.description: A statement is independent of a theory if neither it nor its negation can be proved from the axioms.

topics.title: Gödel's constructible universe
topics.description: Gödel's constructible universe L is an inner model of ZF in which the axiom of choice and GCH hold.

topics.title: Large cardinal
topics.description: Large cardinals are cardinals so big that their existence implies the consistency of ZFC and more.

topics.title: Descriptive set theory
topics.description: Descriptive set theory studies definable sets of reals and their topological and measure-theoretic properties.

topics.title: Inner model
topics.description: An inner model is a transitive class model of set theory contained in the universe V, like L or L[R].

topics.title: Martin's axiom
topics.description: Martin's axiom is a forcing axiom that generalizes the Baire category theorem and implies many consequences of CH.

topics.title: Proper forcing
topics.description: Proper forcing is a class of posets that preserve stationary sets and do not collapse ω₁, useful in iterated forcing.
